`predsci/POT3D`"}],"state":"open","locked":false,"assignee":null,"assignees":[],"milestone":null,"comments":2,"created_at":"2024-12-10T17:42:28Z","updated_at":"2024-12-10T20:10:26Z","closed_at":null,"author_association":"CONTRIBUTOR","active_lock_reason":null,"body":"## Minimal Reproducible Example

```f90
module p
    implicit none
    integer, parameter, private :: mxdim = 3
    type :: rp1d
        real(8), dimension(:), pointer :: f
    end type

    type :: sds
        integer :: ndim
        integer, dimension(mxdim) :: dims
        logical :: hdf32
        type(rp1d), dimension(mxdim) :: scales
        real(8), dimension(:,:,:), pointer :: f
    end type
end module

program main
    use p
    implicit none
    type(sds) :: s
    nullify(s%scales(1)%f)
end program

```

The above program on it's compilation with LFortran raises the below error:
```console
> lfortran test.f90
semantic error: Only a pointer variable symbol can be nullified.
  --> test.f90:21:13
   |
21 |     nullify(s%scales(1)%f)
   |             ^^^^^^^^^^^^^ 


Note: Please report unclear, confusing or incorrect messages as bugs at
https://github.com/lfortran/lfortran/issues.
```

Minimal Reproducible Example

```f90
module p
    implicit none
    integer, parameter, private :: mxdim = 3
    type :: rp1d
        real(8), dimension(:), pointer :: f
    end type

    type :: sds
        integer :: ndim
        integer, dimension(mxdim) :: dims
        logical :: hdf32
        type(rp1d), dimension(mxdim) :: scales
        real(8), dimension(:,:,:), pointer :: f
    end type
end module

program main
    use p
    implicit none
    type(sds) :: s
    nullify(s%scales(1)%f)
end program

```

The above program on it's compilation with LFortran raises the below error:
```console
> lfortran test.f90
semantic error: Only a pointer variable symbol can be nullified.
  --> test.f90:21:13
   |
21 |     nullify(s%scales(1)%f)
   |             ^^^^^^^^^^^^^ 


Note: Please report unclear, confusing or incorrect messages as bugs at
https://github.com/lfortran/lfortran/issues.
```

while the above program works without any issue with GFortran.","reactions":{"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/lfortran/lfortran/issues/5639/reactions","total_count":0,"+1":0,"-1":0,"laugh":0,"hooray":0,"confused":0,"heart":0,"rocket":0,"eyes":0},"timeline_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/lfortran/lfortran/issues/5639/timeline","performed_via_github_app":null,"state_reason":null},"comment":{"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/lfortran/lfortran/issues/comments/2532766639","html_url":"https://github.com/lfortran/lfortran/issues/5639#issuecomment-2532766639","issue_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/lfortran/lfortran/issues/5639","id":2532766639,"node_id":"IC_kwDOCpMuHc6W9vOv","user":{"login":"adit4443ya","id":120598696,"node_id":"U_kgDOBzAwqA","avatar_url":"https://avatars.githubusercontent.com/u/120598696?v=4","gravatar_id":"","url":"https://api.github.com/users/adit4443ya","html_url":"https://github.com/adit4443ya","followers_url":"https://api.github.com/users/adit4443ya/followers","following_url":"https://api.github.com/users/adit4443ya/following{/other_user}","gists_url":"https://api.github.com/users/adit4443ya/gists{/gist_id}","starred_url":"https://api.github.com/users/adit4443ya/starred{/owner}{/repo}","subscriptions_url":"https://api.github.com/users/adit4443ya/subscriptions","organizations_url":"https://api.github.com/users/adit4443ya/orgs","repos_url":"https://api.github.com/users/adit4443ya/repos","events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/adit4443ya/events{/privacy}","received_events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/adit4443ya/received_events","type":"User","user_view_type":"public","site_admin":false},"created_at":"2024-12-10T20:10:56Z","updated_at":"2024-12-10T20:10:56Z","author_association":"CONTRIBUTOR","body":"Any thoughts why this is happening?

After the update, I received the following output:
```shell
             aditya-trivedi   lfortran    main ↓15  ?2 ~1    lfortran ./integration_tests/test_mre.f90  --show-asr
75
--------------The Struct Member is pointer
(TranslationUnit
    (SymbolTable
        1
        {
            main:
                (Program
                    (SymbolTable
                        5
                        {
                            1_rp1d_f:
                                (ExternalSymbol
                                    5
                                    1_rp1d_f
                                    3 f
                                    rp1d
                                    []
                                    f
                                    Public
                                ),
                            1_sds_scales:
                                (ExternalSymbol
                                    5
                                    1_sds_scales
                                    4 scales
                                    sds
                                    []
                                    scales
                                    Public
                                ),
                            rp1d:
                                (ExternalSymbol
                                    5
                                    rp1d
                                    2 rp1d
                                    p
                                    []
                                    rp1d
                                    Public
                                ),
                            s:
                                (Variable
                                    5
                                    s
                                    []
                                    Local
                                    ()
                                    ()
                                    Default
                                    (StructType
                                        5 sds
                                    )
                                    ()
                                    Source
                                    Public
                                    Required
                                    .false.
                                ),
                            sds:
                                (ExternalSymbol
                                    5
                                    sds
                                    2 sds
                                    p
                                    []
                                    sds
                                    Public
                                )
                        })
                    main
                    [p]
                    [(Nullify
                        [5 1_rp1d_f]    //This is what i tried to achieve in ASR
                    )]
                ),    
```
I believe this should work as in `visit_Nullify()`. The line `Vec<ASR::symbol_t*> arg_vec;` needs a pointer-type symbol, and accordingly, my diff goes into this IF block
```diff
+if (tmp_expr->type == ASR::exprType::StructInstanceMember && ASR::is_a<ASR::Pointer_t>(*ASR::down_cast<ASR::StructInstanceMember_t>(tmp_expr)->m_type)) {
+                    std::cout<<"--------------The Struct Member is pointer"<<std::endl;
+                    arg_vec.push_back(al, ASR::down_cast<ASR::StructInstanceMember_t>(tmp_expr)->m_m);
+                }
```
, resulting in `arg_vec` having `[5 1_rp1d_f]` as a value, which is a `symbol_t*`.","reactions":{"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/lfortran/lfortran/issues/comments/2534190101/reactions","total_count":0,"+1":0,"-1":0,"laugh":0,"hooray":0,"confused":0,"heart":0,"rocket":0,"eyes":0},"performed_via_github_app":null}}

Minimal Reproducible Example

```f90
module p
    implicit none
    integer, parameter, private :: mxdim = 3
    type :: rp1d
        real(8), dimension(:), pointer :: f
    end type

    type :: sds
        integer :: ndim
        integer, dimension(mxdim) :: dims
        logical :: hdf32
        type(rp1d), dimension(mxdim) :: scales
        real(8), dimension(:,:,:), pointer :: f
    end type
end module

program main
    use p
    implicit none
    type(sds) :: s
    nullify(s%scales(1)%f)
end program

```

The above program on it's compilation with LFortran raises the below error:
```console
> lfortran test.f90
semantic error: Only a pointer variable symbol can be nullified.
  --> test.f90:21:13
   |
21 |     nullify(s%scales(1)%f)
   |             ^^^^^^^^^^^^^ 


Note: Please report unclear, confusing or incorrect messages as bugs at
https://github.com/lfortran/lfortran/issues.
```

while the above program works without any issue with Minimal Reproducible Example

```f90
module p
    implicit none
    integer, parameter, private :: mxdim = 3
    type :: rp1d
        real(8), dimension(:), pointer :: f
    end type

    type :: sds
        integer :: ndim
        integer, dimension(mxdim) :: dims
        logical :: hdf32
        type(rp1d), dimension(mxdim) :: scales
        real(8), dimension(:,:,:), pointer :: f
    end type
end module

program main
    use p
    implicit none
    type(sds) :: s
    nullify(s%scales(1)%f)
end program

```

The above program on it's compilation with LFortran raises the below error:
```console
> lfortran test.f90
semantic error: Only a pointer variable symbol can be nullified.
  --> test.f90:21:13
   |
21 |     nullify(s%scales(1)%f)
   |             ^^^^^^^^^^^^^ 


Note: Please report unclear, confusing or incorrect messages as bugs at
https://github.com/lfortran/lfortran/issues.
```

while the above program works without any issue with GFortran.","reactions":{"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/lfortran/lfortran/issues/5639/reactions","total_count":0,"+1":0,"-1":0,"laugh":0,"hooray":0,"confused":0,"heart":0,"rocket":0,"eyes":0},"timeline_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/lfortran/lfortran/issues/5639/timeline","performed_via_github_app":null,"state_reason":null},"comment":{"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/lfortran/lfortran/issues/comments/2536868373","html_url":"https://github.com/lfortran/lfortran/issues/5639#issuecomment-2536868373","issue_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/lfortran/lfortran/issues/5639","id":2536868373,"node_id":"IC_kwDOCpMuHc6XNYoV","user":{"login":"adit4443ya","id":120598696,"node_id":"U_kgDOBzAwqA","avatar_url":"https://avatars.githubusercontent.com/u/120598696?v=4","gravatar_id":"","url":"https://api.github.com/users/adit4443ya","html_url":"https://github.com/adit4443ya","followers_url":"https://api.github.com/users/adit4443ya/followers","following_url":"https://api.github.com/users/adit4443ya/following{/other_user}","gists_url":"https://api.github.com/users/adit4443ya/gists{/gist_id}","starred_url":"https://api.github.com/users/adit4443ya/starred{/owner}{/repo}","subscriptions_url":"https://api.github.com/users/adit4443ya/subscriptions","organizations_url":"https://api.github.com/users/adit4443ya/orgs","repos_url":"https://api.github.com/users/adit4443ya/repos","events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/adit4443ya/events{/privacy}","received_events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/adit4443ya/received_events","type":"User","user_view_type":"public","site_admin":false},"created_at":"2024-12-11T18:56:33Z","updated_at":"2024-12-11T18:56:33Z","author_association":"CONTRIBUTOR","body":"In this ASR Generated

> You can see that there are two `nullify` nodes:
> 
> ```clojure

>                     (Nullify

>                     )
> ```
> even though they apply on different instances of `scaled_data_structure`, but produce the same ASR, which I think is incorrect. Something is wrong there.

I see that good catch,, 
I guess this is because of the fact that there is only one symbol for both the StructInstanceMember 
There must be something like 
1_s_scaled_data_structure_one_d_real_pointer and 
1_s1_scaled_data_structure_one_d_real_pointer

in the ASR so that we can differentiate between the symbols of two different variables declared in the Program

Then it would be 

> ```clojure
>                     (Nullify
>                         [4 1_s_scaled_data_structure_one_d_real_pointer]
>                     )
>                     (Nullify
>                         [4 1_s1_scaled_data_structure_one_d_real_pointer]
>                     )
> ```

PR adds a Nullify visitor in the fortran backend
```fortran
 aditya-trivedi   lfortran    fortran ≢    cat ./integration_tests/nullify_02.f90 
program nullify_02
implicit none

   real, pointer :: p1
   real, target :: t1 
   integer, pointer :: p2
   integer, target :: t2
   
   p1=>t1
   p1 = 1
   p2=>t2
   p2 = 2.
   nullify(p1, p2) 
   
   
end program nullify_02
 aditya-trivedi   lfortran    fortran ≢    lfortran ./integration_tests/nullify_02.f90 --show-fortran --show-stacktrace
program nullify_02
implicit none
real(4), pointer :: p1
integer(4), pointer :: p2
real(4) :: t1
integer(4) :: t2
p1 => t1
p1 = real(1, kind=4)
p2 => t2
p2 = int(2.00000000e+00, kind=4)
nullify (p1, p2)

6 | p1 => t1
      |      1
Error: Pointer assignment target is neither TARGET nor POINTER at (1)
CMakeFiles/nullify_01.dir/nullify_01.f90.o.tmp.f90:7:6:

    7 | p2 => t1
      |      1
Error: Pointer assignment target is neither TARGET nor POINTER at (1)
```
It's due to the fact the in fortran backend 
`integer, target :: t2` is transformed into 
`integer(4) :: t2`

> * Let's use `expr_type` and check that each argument is a `Pointer` (I don't know if we can just use `is_a<Pointer_t>` on the type, or if we need to call an auxiliary function that will determine if the type is a Pointer, as the Pointer node might not be an upper level node, say with Array or other more complex types). If it is not a pointer, then we give a nice error message.

I agree on this point but expr_type don't have `Pointer` in enum ,,, as you said it's not upper level node 
In this case upper level node is the `StructInstanceMember` so we need to get from this that if it's a pointer or not 
same with `Var_t` ....

PR adds a Nullify visitor in the fortran backend
```fortran
 aditya-trivedi   lfortran    fortran ≢    cat ./integration_tests/nullify_02.f90 
program nullify_02
implicit none

   real, pointer :: p1
   real, target :: t1 
   integer, pointer :: p2
   integer, target :: t2
   
   p1=>t1
   p1 = 1
   p2=>t2
   p2 = 2.
   nullify(p1, p2) 
   
   
end program nullify_02
 aditya-trivedi   lfortran    fortran ≢    lfortran ./integration_tests/nullify_02.f90 --show-fortran --show-stacktrace
program nullify_02
implicit none
real(4), pointer :: p1
integer(4), pointer :: p2
real(4) :: t1
integer(4) :: t2
p1 => t1
p1 = real(1, kind=4)
p2 => t2
p2 = int(2.00000000e+00, kind=4)
nullify (p1, p2)
end program nullify_02

For this case 
```fortran
module p
    implicit none

    type :: scaled_data_structure
        real, dimension(:), pointer :: one_d_real_pointer
    end type
end module

program nullify_04
    use p, only : scaled_data_structure
    implicit none
    type(scaled_data_structure) :: s
    type(scaled_data_structure) :: s2
    nullify(s%one_d_real_pointer)
    nullify(s2%one_d_real_pointer)
end program

PR adds a Nullify visitor in the fortran backend
```fortran
 aditya-trivedi   lfortran    fortran ≢    cat ./integration_tests/nullify_02.f90 
program nullify_02
implicit none

   real, pointer :: p1
   real, target :: t1 
   integer, pointer :: p2
   integer, target :: t2
   
   p1=>t1
   p1 = 1
   p2=>t2
   p2 = 2.
   nullify(p1, p2) 
   
   
end program nullify_02
 aditya-trivedi   lfortran    fortran ≢    lfortran ./integration_tests/nullify_02.f90 --show-fortran --show-stacktrace
program nullify_02
implicit none
real(4), pointer :: p1
integer(4), pointer :: p2
real(4) :: t1
integer(4) :: t2
p1 => t1
p1 = real(1, kind=4)
p2 => t2
p2 = int(2.00000000e+00, kind=4)
nullify (p1, p2)
end program nullify_02

```","reactions":{"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/lfortran/lfortran/issues/5665/reactions","total_count":0,"+1":0,"-1":0,"laugh":0,"hooray":0,"confused":0,"heart":0,"rocket":0,"eyes":0},"timeline_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/lfortran/lfortran/issues/5665/timeline","performed_via_github_app":null,"state_reason":null},"comment":{"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/lfortran/lfortran/issues/comments/2541875321","html_url":"https://github.com/lfortran/lfortran/pull/5665#issuecomment-2541875321","issue_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/lfortran/lfortran/issues/5665","id":2541875321,"node_id":"IC_kwDOCpMuHc6XgfB5","user":{"login":"adit4443ya","id":120598696,"node_id":"U_kgDOBzAwqA","avatar_url":"https://avatars.githubusercontent.com/u/120598696?v=4","gravatar_id":"","url":"https://api.github.com/users/adit4443ya","html_url":"https://github.com/adit4443ya","followers_url":"https://api.github.com/users/adit4443ya/followers","following_url":"https://api.github.com/users/adit4443ya/following{/other_user}","gists_url":"https://api.github.com/users/adit4443ya/gists{/gist_id}","starred_url":"https://api.github.com/users/adit4443ya/starred{/owner}{/repo}","subscriptions_url":"https://api.github.com/users/adit4443ya/subscriptions","organizations_url":"https://api.github.com/users/adit4443ya/orgs","repos_url":"https://api.github.com/users/adit4443ya/repos","events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/adit4443ya/events{/privacy}","received_events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/adit4443ya/received_events","type":"User","user_view_type":"public","site_admin":false},"created_at":"2024-12-13T17:03:28Z","updated_at":"2024-12-13T17:03:28Z","author_association":"CONTRIBUTOR","body":"I tried to set the default value of `target_attr` to `true`, but `Variable_t` has many dependencies, hence we need a track of where `target_attr` should be false. For instance, we cannot use `real(4), pointer, target :: p1`; thus, we must ensure `target_attr` is false whenever we encounter a pointer.

One solution is to implement a single visitor function just before ASR generation to set `target_attr` to false whenever `ASR::is_a<Pointer_t>(*var->m_type)` is true.

Tell me how that sounds @Pranavchiku @gxyd ","reactions":{"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/lfortran/lfortran/issues/comments/2541875321/reactions","total_count":0,"+1":0,"-1":0,"laugh":0,"hooray":0,"confused":0,"heart":0,"rocket":0,"eyes":0},"performed_via_github_app":null}}

program nullify_01
implicit none

   integer, pointer :: p1, p2
   integer, target :: t1 
   
   p1=>t1
   p2=>t1
   p1 = 1
   nullify(p1, p2)
   
end program nullify_01
 aditya-trivedi   lfortran    main ≡    lfortran integration_tests/nullify_01.f90 --show-asr

    (SymbolTable

                                    ()
                                    Source
                                    Public
                                    Required
                                    .false.
                                )

                    nullify_01

```fortran
 aditya-trivedi   lfortran    fortran ≢    cat ./integration_tests/nullify_02.f90 
program nullify_02
implicit none

   real, pointer :: p1
   real, target :: t1 
   integer, pointer :: p2
   integer, target :: t2
   
   p1=>t1
   p1 = 1
   p2=>t2
   p2 = 2.
   nullify(p1, p2) 
   
   
end program nullify_02
 aditya-trivedi   lfortran    fortran ≢    lfortran ./integration_tests/nullify_02.f90 --show-fortran --show-stacktrace
program nullify_02
implicit none
real(4), pointer :: p1
integer(4), pointer :: p2
real(4) :: t1
integer(4) :: t2
p1 => t1
p1 = real(1, kind=4)
p2 => t2
p2 = int(2.00000000e+00, kind=4)
nullify (p1, p2)
end program nullify_02

   p1=>t1
   p2=>t1
   p1 = 1
   nullify(p1, p2)
   
end program nullify_01

In this PR i have changed 
```fortran
integer, dimension(2) :: drratio=0 --------> integer,dimension(2) :: drratio
real, dimension(2) :: drratior=0.0 ---------> real, dimension(2) :: drratior
```
On ASR level 
Which prevents this llvm error 
```clojure
aditya-trivedi   lfortran    main ≡    lfortran integration_tests/array_init_with_scalar.f90 --show-llvm
; ModuleID = 'LFortran'
source_filename = "LFortran"

@drratio = global [2 x i32] zeroinitializer
@drratior = global [2 x float] zeroinitializer

define i32 @main(i32 %0, i8** %1) {
.entry:
  call void @_lpython_call_initial_functions(i32 %0, i8** %1)
  call void @_lpython_free_argv()
  br label %return

return:                                           ; preds = %.entry
  ret i32 0
}

declare void @_lpython_call_initial_functions(i32, i8**)

declare void @_lpython_free_argv()

Minimal Reproducible Example

For the below Fortran program:
```f90
module meshdef
    implicit none
    integer, dimension(2) :: drratio=0 ! if this was [1, 2] it works
end module

program main
    implicit none
end program main

```

on executing it with LFortran, I get the below error:
```console
> lfortran test.f90
; ModuleID = 'LFortran'
source_filename = "LFortran"

@drratio = global [2 x i32] zeroinitializer

define i32 @main(i32 %0, i8** %1) {
.entry:
  call void @_lpython_call_initial_functions(i32 %0, i8** %1)
  call void @_lpython_free_argv()
  br label %return

return:                                           ; preds = %.entry
  ret i32 0
}

declare void @_lpython_call_initial_functions(i32, i8**)

declare void @_lpython_free_argv()
asr_to_llvm: module failed verification. Error:
Global variable initializer type does not match global variable type!
[2 x i32]* @drratio

code generation error: asr_to_llvm: module failed verification. Error:
Global variable initializer type does not match global variable type!
[2 x i32]* @drratio



Note: Please report unclear, confusing or incorrect messages as bugs at
https://github.com/lfortran/lfortran/issues.
```

while the above program works without error with GFortran.","reactions":{"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/lfortran/lfortran/issues/5690/reactions","total_count":0,"+1":0,"-1":0,"laugh":0,"hooray":0,"confused":0,"heart":0,"rocket":0,"eyes":0},"timeline_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/lfortran/lfortran/issues/5690/timeline","performed_via_github_app":null,"state_reason":null},"comment":{"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/lfortran/lfortran/issues/comments/2543839784","html_url":"https://github.com/lfortran/lfortran/issues/5690#issuecomment-2543839784","issue_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/lfortran/lfortran/issues/5690","id":2543839784,"node_id":"IC_kwDOCpMuHc6Xn-oo","user":{"login":"adit4443ya","id":120598696,"node_id":"U_kgDOBzAwqA","avatar_url":"https://avatars.githubusercontent.com/u/120598696?v=4","gravatar_id":"","url":"https://api.github.com/users/adit4443ya","html_url":"https://github.com/adit4443ya","followers_url":"https://api.github.com/users/adit4443ya/followers","following_url":"https://api.github.com/users/adit4443ya/following{/other_user}","gists_url":"https://api.github.com/users/adit4443ya/gists{/gist_id}","starred_url":"https://api.github.com/users/adit4443ya/starred{/owner}{/repo}","subscriptions_url":"https://api.github.com/users/adit4443ya/subscriptions","organizations_url":"https://api.github.com/users/adit4443ya/orgs","repos_url":"https://api.github.com/users/adit4443ya/repos","events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/adit4443ya/events{/privacy}","received_events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/adit4443ya/received_events","type":"User","user_view_type":"public","site_admin":false},"created_at":"2024-12-15T11:41:58Z","updated_at":"2024-12-15T11:41:58Z","author_association":"CONTRIBUTOR","body":"> But, I think the correct fix should be to handle this case in the ASR, where we convert the above declaration to `integer, dimension(2) :: drratio`. Thanks @kmr-srbh

#5690 
In this PR i have changed 
```fortran
integer, dimension(2) :: drratio=0 --------> integer,dimension(2) :: drratio
real, dimension(2) :: drratior=0.0 ---------> real, dimension(2) :: drratior
```
On ASR level 
Which prevents this llvm error 
```clojure
aditya-trivedi   lfortran    main ≡    lfortran integration_tests/array_init_with_scalar.f90 --show-llvm
; ModuleID = 'LFortran'
source_filename = "LFortran"

@drratio = global [2 x i32] zeroinitializer
@drratior = global [2 x float] zeroinitializer

define i32 @main(i32 %0, i8** %1) {
.entry:
  call void @_lpython_call_initial_functions(i32 %0, i8** %1)
  call void @_lpython_free_argv()
  br label %return

return:                                           ; preds = %.entry
  ret i32 0
}

declare void @_lpython_call_initial_functions(i32, i8**)

declare void @_lpython_free_argv()

#5690 
In this PR i have changed 
```fortran
integer, dimension(2) :: drratio=0 --------> integer,dimension(2) :: drratio
real, dimension(2) :: drratior=0.0 ---------> real, dimension(2) :: drratior
```
On ASR level 
Which prevents this llvm error 
```clojure
aditya-trivedi   lfortran    main ≡    lfortran integration_tests/array_init_with_scalar.f90 --show-llvm
; ModuleID = 'LFortran'
source_filename = "LFortran"

@drratio = global [2 x i32] zeroinitializer
@drratior = global [2 x float] zeroinitializer

define i32 @main(i32 %0, i8** %1) {
.entry:
  call void @_lpython_call_initial_functions(i32 %0, i8** %1)
  call void @_lpython_free_argv()
  br label %return

return:                                           ; preds = %.entry
  ret i32 0
}

declare void @_lpython_call_initial_functions(i32, i8**)

declare void @_lpython_free_argv()

#5690 
In this PR i have changed 
```fortran
integer, dimension(2) :: drratio=0 --------> integer,dimension(2) :: drratio
real, dimension(2) :: drratior=0.0 ---------> real, dimension(2) :: drratior
```
On ASR level 
Which prevents this llvm error 
```clojure
aditya-trivedi   lfortran    main ≡    lfortran integration_tests/array_init_with_scalar.f90 --show-llvm
; ModuleID = 'LFortran'
source_filename = "LFortran"

@drratio = global [2 x i32] zeroinitializer
@drratior = global [2 x float] zeroinitializer

define i32 @main(i32 %0, i8** %1) {
.entry:
  call void @_lpython_call_initial_functions(i32 %0, i8** %1)
  call void @_lpython_free_argv()
  br label %return

return:                                           ; preds = %.entry
  ret i32 0
}

declare void @_lpython_call_initial_functions(i32, i8**)

declare void @_lpython_free_argv()
asr_to_llvm: module failed verification. Error:
Global variable initializer type does not match global variable type!
[2 x i32]* @drratio
Global variable initializer type does not match global variable type!
[2 x float]* @drratior

code generation error: asr_to_llvm: module failed verification. Error:
Global variable initializer type does not match global variable type!
[2 x i32]* @drratio
Global variable initializer type does not match global variable type!
[2 x float]* @drratior



Note: Please report unclear, confusing or incorrect messages as bugs at
https://github.com/lfortran/lfortran/issues.

```

#5690 
In this PR i have changed 
```fortran
integer, dimension(2) :: drratio=0 --------> integer,dimension(2) :: drratio
real, dimension(2) :: drratior=0.0 ---------> real, dimension(2) :: drratior
```
On ASR level 
Which prevents this llvm error 
```clojure
aditya-trivedi   lfortran    main ≡    lfortran integration_tests/array_init_with_scalar.f90 --show-llvm
; ModuleID = 'LFortran'
source_filename = "LFortran"

@drratio = global [2 x i32] zeroinitializer
@drratior = global [2 x float] zeroinitializer

define i32 @main(i32 %0, i8** %1) {
.entry:
  call void @_lpython_call_initial_functions(i32 %0, i8** %1)
  call void @_lpython_free_argv()
  br label %return

return:                                           ; preds = %.entry
  ret i32 0
}

declare void @_lpython_call_initial_functions(i32, i8**)

declare void @_lpython_free_argv()
asr_to_llvm: module failed verification. Error:
Global variable initializer type does not match global variable type!
[2 x i32]* @drratio
Global variable initializer type does not match global variable type!
[2 x float]* @drratior

code generation error: asr_to_llvm: module failed verification. Error:
Global variable initializer type does not match global variable type!
[2 x i32]* @drratio
Global variable initializer type does not match global variable type!
[2 x float]* @drratior



Note: Please report unclear, confusing or incorrect messages as bugs at
https://github.com/lfortran/lfortran/issues.

```

> 
> > I'm confused, both the LLVM IR (before and after this PR) seems exactly the same. How is the issue fixed here?
> 
> ```clojure
>  aditya-trivedi   lfortran    main ↓2    lfortran integration_tests/array_init_with_scalar.f90 --show-llvm
> ; ModuleID = 'LFortran'
> source_filename = "LFortran"
> 
> @drratio = global [2 x i32] zeroinitializer
> @drratior = global [2 x float] zeroinitializer
> 
> define i32 @main(i32 %0, i8** %1) {
> .entry:
>   call void @_lpython_call_initial_functions(i32 %0, i8** %1)
>   call void @_lpython_free_argv()
>   br label %return
> 
> return:                                           ; preds = %.entry
>   ret i32 0
> }
> 
> declare void @_lpython_call_initial_functions(i32, i8**)
> 
> declare void @_lpython_free_argv()
> asr_to_llvm: module failed verification. Error:
> Global variable initializer type does not match global variable type!
> [2 x i32]* @drratio
> Global variable initializer type does not match global variable type!
> [2 x float]* @drratior
> 
> code generation error: asr_to_llvm: module failed verification. Error:
> Global variable initializer type does not match global variable type!
> [2 x i32]* @drratio
> Global variable initializer type does not match global variable type!
> [2 x float]* @drratior
> 
> 
> 
> Note: Please report unclear, confusing or incorrect messages as bugs at
> https://github.com/lfortran/lfortran/issues.
> ```


As you can see here , there is type mismatch here , as LLVM initializes the variable with `zeroinitializer` instead of [i32 0, i32 0], raising this error

#5690 
In this PR i have changed 
```fortran
integer, dimension(2) :: drratio=0 --------> integer,dimension(2) :: drratio
real, dimension(2) :: drratior=0.0 ---------> real, dimension(2) :: drratior
```
On ASR level 
Which prevents this llvm error 
```clojure
aditya-trivedi   lfortran    main ≡    lfortran integration_tests/array_init_with_scalar.f90 --show-llvm
; ModuleID = 'LFortran'
source_filename = "LFortran"

@drratio = global [2 x i32] zeroinitializer
@drratior = global [2 x float] zeroinitializer

define i32 @main(i32 %0, i8** %1) {
.entry:
  call void @_lpython_call_initial_functions(i32 %0, i8** %1)
  call void @_lpython_free_argv()
  br label %return

return:                                           ; preds = %.entry
  ret i32 0
}

declare void @_lpython_call_initial_functions(i32, i8**)

declare void @_lpython_free_argv()
asr_to_llvm: module failed verification. Error:
Global variable initializer type does not match global variable type!
[2 x i32]* @drratio
Global variable initializer type does not match global variable type!
[2 x float]* @drratior

code generation error: asr_to_llvm: module failed verification. Error:
Global variable initializer type does not match global variable type!
[2 x i32]* @drratio
Global variable initializer type does not match global variable type!
[2 x float]* @drratior



Note: Please report unclear, confusing or incorrect messages as bugs at
https://github.com/lfortran/lfortran/issues.

```

#5690 
In this PR i have changed 
```fortran
integer, dimension(2) :: drratio=0 --------> integer,dimension(2) :: drratio
real, dimension(2) :: drratior=0.0 ---------> real, dimension(2) :: drratior
```
On ASR level 
Which prevents this llvm error 
```clojure
aditya-trivedi   lfortran    main ≡    lfortran integration_tests/array_init_with_scalar.f90 --show-llvm
; ModuleID = 'LFortran'
source_filename = "LFortran"

@drratio = global [2 x i32] zeroinitializer
@drratior = global [2 x float] zeroinitializer

define i32 @main(i32 %0, i8** %1) {
.entry:
  call void @_lpython_call_initial_functions(i32 %0, i8** %1)
  call void @_lpython_free_argv()
  br label %return

return:                                           ; preds = %.entry
  ret i32 0
}

declare void @_lpython_call_initial_functions(i32, i8**)

declare void @_lpython_free_argv()
asr_to_llvm: module failed verification. Error:
Global variable initializer type does not match global variable type!
[2 x i32]* @drratio
Global variable initializer type does not match global variable type!
[2 x float]* @drratior

code generation error: asr_to_llvm: module failed verification. Error:
Global variable initializer type does not match global variable type!
[2 x i32]* @drratio
Global variable initializer type does not match global variable type!
[2 x float]* @drratior



Note: Please report unclear, confusing or incorrect messages as bugs at
https://github.com/lfortran/lfortran/issues.

```

#5690 
In this PR i have changed 
```fortran
integer, dimension(2) :: drratio=0 --------> integer,dimension(2) :: drratio
real, dimension(2) :: drratior=0.0 ---------> real, dimension(2) :: drratior
```
On ASR level 
Which prevents this llvm error 
```clojure
aditya-trivedi   lfortran    main ≡    lfortran integration_tests/array_init_with_scalar.f90 --show-llvm
; ModuleID = 'LFortran'
source_filename = "LFortran"

@drratio = global [2 x i32] zeroinitializer
@drratior = global [2 x float] zeroinitializer

define i32 @main(i32 %0, i8** %1) {
.entry:
  call void @_lpython_call_initial_functions(i32 %0, i8** %1)
  call void @_lpython_free_argv()
  br label %return

return:                                           ; preds = %.entry
  ret i32 0
}

declare void @_lpython_call_initial_functions(i32, i8**)

declare void @_lpython_free_argv()
asr_to_llvm: module failed verification. Error:
Global variable initializer type does not match global variable type!
[2 x i32]* @drratio
Global variable initializer type does not match global variable type!
[2 x float]* @drratior

Global variable initializer type does not match global variable type!
[2 x i32]* @drratio
Global variable initializer type does not match global variable type!
[2 x float]* @drratior

```

I agree with your arguments , But only reason why i chose this approach is, if we make that check in backend then we are iterating through each element in LLVM backend again, which will be slower if the array is large and can hinder the speed,
But if we handle this in ASR , then it would save us cost of iterations, 

> ```fortran
> module expr2
> integer :: x(2) = [0,0]
> end
> ```

Also I would incorporate this case in this PR too,

Let me know your thoughts on this
@Thirumalai-Shaktivel @kmr-srbh on this ","reactions":{"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/lfortran/lfortran/issues/comments/2544031880/reactions","total_count":0,"+1":0,"-1":0,"laugh":0,"hooray":0,"confused":0,"heart":0,"rocket":0,"eyes":0},"performed_via_github_app":null}}

PR adds a Nullify visitor in the fortran backend
```fortran
 aditya-trivedi   lfortran    fortran ≢    cat ./integration_tests/nullify_02.f90 
program nullify_02
implicit none

   real, pointer :: p1
   real, target :: t1 
   integer, pointer :: p2
   integer, target :: t2
   
   p1=>t1
   p1 = 1
   p2=>t2
   p2 = 2.
   nullify(p1, p2) 
   
   
end program nullify_02
 aditya-trivedi   lfortran    fortran ≢    lfortran ./integration_tests/nullify_02.f90 --show-fortran --show-stacktrace
program nullify_02
implicit none
real(4), pointer :: p1
integer(4), pointer :: p2
real(4) :: t1
integer(4) :: t2
p1 => t1
p1 = real(1, kind=4)
p2 => t2
p2 = int(2.00000000e+00, kind=4)
nullify (p1, p2)
end program nullify_02

Minimal Reproducible Example

For the below Fortran program:
```f90
program POT3D
    use iso_fortran_env, only : OUTPUT_UNIT
    implicit none
    print *, OUTPUT_UNIT
    call FLUSH(OUTPUT_UNIT) ! I used 'call FLUSH(OUTPUT_UNIT)' instead of just 'FLUSH(OUTPUT_UNIT)'
end

```

on it's execution with LFortran, I get the below error:
```console

I think this is because we didn't connected that unit number with output buffer 
This compiles well
```fortran
program POT3D  
    use iso_fortran_env, only : OUTPUT_UNIT  
    implicit none  
    
    open(OUTPUT_UNIT, file="data.txt")
   
    flush(OUTPUT_UNIT)  
end program POT3D
```","reactions":{"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/lfortran/lfortran/issues/comments/2562717025/reactions","total_count":0,"+1":0,"-1":0,"laugh":0,"hooray":0,"confused":0,"heart":0,"rocket":0,"eyes":0},"performed_via_github_app":null}}

example from https://gist.github.com/hakostra/ac5ed9279136fe1e7f6f217f2561f08e, modified to compile (to workaround bugs #5870, #4248): https://gist.github.com/certik/924cf554b0144f00d15937970310fab7 works like this:
```console
$ lfortran --cpp -D_USE_DO_CONCURRENT_ a.f90 -o a.out
$ lfortran --cpp  a.f90 -o a.out 
```
But fails to compile when you add `--openmp` to either line above:
```console
$ lfortran --cpp -D_USE_DO_CONCURRENT_ --openmp a.f90 -o x.omp
zsh: segmentation fault  lfortran --cpp -D_USE_DO_CONCURRENT_ --openmp a.f90 -o x.omp
```
LFortran was compiled in Release mode using `./build_release.sh`. In Debug mode we get:
```

clang: error: linker command failed with exit code 1 (use -v to see invocation)
The command 'clang -o x.omp a.tmp.o  -L"/Users/ondrej/repos/lfortran/src/bin/../runtime" -Wl,-rpath,"/Users/ondrej/repos/lfortran/src/bin/../runtime" -llfortran_runtime -lm' failed.
Tip: If there is a linker issue, switch the linker using --linker=<CC> option or create an environment variable `export LFORTRAN_LINKER=<CC>`, where CC is clang or gcc
Also, if required use --linker-path=<PATH>, where PATH has location to look for the linker execuatable
```

We should tell the user to use the following option:
```
$ lfortran --cpp -D_USE_DO_CONCURRENT_ --openmp a.f90 -o x.omp --openmp-lib-dir=$CONDA_PREFIX/lib
Undefined symbols for architecture arm64:
  "_zatan2", referenced from:
      _lcompilers_function in a.tmp.o
ld: symbol(s) not found for architecture arm64
clang: error: linker command failed with exit code 1 (use -v to see invocation)
The command 'clang -o x.omp a.tmp.o  -L"/Users/ondrej/repos/lfortran/src/bin/../runtime" -Wl,-rpath,"/Users/ondrej/repos/lfortran/src/bin/../runtime" -llfortran_runtime -lm -L/Users/ondrej/miniforge3/envs/lf/lib -Wl,-rpath,/Users/ondrej/miniforge3/envs/lf/lib -lomp' failed.
Tip: If there is a linker issue, switch the linker using --linker=<CC> option or create an environment variable `export LFORTRAN_LINKER=<CC>`, where CC is clang or gcc
Also, if required use --linker-path=<PATH>, where PATH has location to look for the linker execuatable
```

It still fails with a linker error, the issue is from calling a user function `zatan2` (declared in the "contains" section of the main program) from inside the `do concurrent` body.

We can comment out the `zatan2` call, then this works and takes about 35s to run:
```
lfortran --cpp -D_USE_DO_CONCURRENT_ --openmp a.f90 -o x.omp --openmp-lib-dir=$CONDA_PREFIX/lib
```
But the following runs almost instantly (0.4s), so this is almost for sure a bug with OpenMP source code:
```
lfortran --cpp  --openmp a.f90 -o x.omp --openmp-lib-dir=$CONDA_PREFIX/lib
```

Bugs to isolate and fix:
* [ ] Segfault in Release mode
* [ ] Better error message in Debug mode
* [ ] User (pure) function inside `do concurrent` body
* [ ] Fix OpenMP input to run like `do concurrent`, something is wrong there
* [ ] Also fix https://github.com/lfortran/lfortran/issues/5870 and https://github.com/lfortran/lfortran/issues/4248 so that no workarounds are needed","reactions":{"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/lfortran/lfortran/issues/5871/reactions","total_count":0,"+1":0,"-1":0,"laugh":0,"hooray":0,"confused":0,"heart":0,"rocket":0,"eyes":0},"timeline_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/lfortran/lfortran/issues/5871/timeline","performed_via_github_app":null,"state_reason":null},"comment":{"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/lfortran/lfortran/issues/comments/2565649784","html_url":"https://github.com/lfortran/lfortran/issues/5871#issuecomment-2565649784","issue_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/lfortran/lfortran/issues/5871","id":2565649784,"node_id":"IC_kwDOCpMuHc6Y7LV4","user":{"login":"adit4443ya","id":120598696,"node_id":"U_kgDOBzAwqA","avatar_url":"https://avatars.githubusercontent.com/u/120598696?v=4","gravatar_id":"","url":"https://api.github.com/users/adit4443ya","html_url":"https://github.com/adit4443ya","followers_url":"https://api.github.com/users/adit4443ya/followers","following_url":"https://api.github.com/users/adit4443ya/following{/other_user}","gists_url":"https://api.github.com/users/adit4443ya/gists{/gist_id}","starred_url":"https://api.github.com/users/adit4443ya/starred{/owner}{/repo}","subscriptions_url":"https://api.github.com/users/adit4443ya/subscriptions","organizations_url":"https://api.github.com/users/adit4443ya/orgs","repos_url":"https://api.github.com/users/adit4443ya/repos","events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/adit4443ya/events{/privacy}","received_events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/adit4443ya/received_events","type":"User","user_view_type":"public","site_admin":false},"created_at":"2024-12-30T15:51:32Z","updated_at":"2024-12-30T15:51:32Z","author_association":"CONTRIBUTOR","body":"I Got the what's hindering in here for

It is because there is change in ASR ,,, 
We have not tested Collapse feature when it's in do loop 
MRE for this is 
```fortran
program openmp_38
    use omp_lib
! declare variables as required
    integer :: ny, nx, nz  , i,j
    integer :: iy, ix, iz
    ny=3
    nx=4
    nz=5
do j=1,2
do i =1,3
#ifdef _USE_DO_CONCURRENT_
do concurrent( iy=1:ny, ix=1:nx , iz=1:nz)
#else
!$omp parallel do collapse(3) private(iy, ix, iz)
do iy = 1, ny
    do ix = 1, nx
        do iz = 1, nz
#endif
            print *,"iy->", iy,"ix->", ix,"iz->", iz
#ifdef _USE_DO_CONCURRENT_
end do 

#else 
        end do
    end do
end do
!$omp end parallel do
#endif
end do
end do
end program
```

Here the level till which this !$omp directive is nested is not handled 
And hence there is null body for doconcurrent which is formed at ASR level

openmp"}],"state":"open","locked":false,"assignee":null,"assignees":[],"milestone":null,"comments":2,"created_at":"2024-12-29T19:36:34Z","updated_at":"2024-12-30T16:22:44Z","closed_at":null,"author_association":"CONTRIBUTOR","active_lock_reason":null,"body":"The example from https://gist.github.com/hakostra/ac5ed9279136fe1e7f6f217f2561f08e, modified to compile (to workaround bugs #5870, #4248): https://gist.github.com/certik/924cf554b0144f00d15937970310fab7 works like this:
```console
$ lfortran --cpp -D_USE_DO_CONCURRENT_ a.f90 -o a.out
$ lfortran --cpp  a.f90 -o a.out 
```
But fails to compile when you add `--openmp` to either line above:
```console
$ lfortran --cpp -D_USE_DO_CONCURRENT_ --openmp a.f90 -o x.omp
zsh: segmentation fault  lfortran --cpp -D_USE_DO_CONCURRENT_ --openmp a.f90 -o x.omp
```
LFortran was compiled in Release mode using `./build_release.sh`. In Debug mode we get:
```
$ lfortran --cpp -D_USE_DO_CONCURRENT_ --openmp a.f90 -o x.omp
Undefined symbols for architecture arm64:
  "_GOMP_barrier", referenced from:
      _lcompilers_function in a.tmp.o
      _lcompilers_function1 in a.tmp.o
  "_GOMP_parallel", referenced from:
      _main in a.tmp.o
      _main in a.tmp.o
  "_omp_get_max_threads", referenced from:
      _lcompilers_function in a.tmp.o
      _lcompilers_function1 in a.tmp.o
  "_omp_get_thread_num", referenced from:
      _lcompilers_function in a.tmp.o
      _lcompilers_function1 in a.tmp.o
  "_zatan2", referenced from:
      _lcompilers_function in a.tmp.o
ld: symbol(s) not found for architecture arm64
clang: error: linker command failed with exit code 1 (use -v to see invocation)
The command 'clang -o x.omp a.tmp.o  -L"/Users/ondrej/repos/lfortran/src/bin/../runtime" -Wl,-rpath,"/Users/ondrej/repos/lfortran/src/bin/../runtime" -llfortran_runtime -lm' failed.
Tip: If there is a linker issue, switch the linker using --linker=<CC> option or create an environment variable `export LFORTRAN_LINKER=<CC>`, where CC is clang or gcc
Also, if required use --linker-path=<PATH>, where PATH has location to look for the linker execuatable
```

We should tell the user to use the following option:
```
$ lfortran --cpp -D_USE_DO_CONCURRENT_ --openmp a.f90 -o x.omp --openmp-lib-dir=$CONDA_PREFIX/lib
Undefined symbols for architecture arm64:
  "_zatan2", referenced from:
      _lcompilers_function in a.tmp.o
ld: symbol(s) not found for architecture arm64
clang: error: linker command failed with exit code 1 (use -v to see invocation)
The command 'clang -o x.omp a.tmp.o  -L"/Users/ondrej/repos/lfortran/src/bin/../runtime" -Wl,-rpath,"/Users/ondrej/repos/lfortran/src/bin/../runtime" -llfortran_runtime -lm -L/Users/ondrej/miniforge3/envs/lf/lib -Wl,-rpath,/Users/ondrej/miniforge3/envs/lf/lib -lomp' failed.
Tip: If there is a linker issue, switch the linker using --linker=<CC> option or create an environment variable `export LFORTRAN_LINKER=<CC>`, where CC is clang or gcc
Also, if required use --linker-path=<PATH>, where PATH has location to look for the linker execuatable
```

It still fails with a linker error, the issue is from calling a user function `zatan2` (declared in the "contains" section of the main program) from inside the `do concurrent` body.

We can comment out the `zatan2` call, then this works and takes about 35s to run:
```
lfortran --cpp -D_USE_DO_CONCURRENT_ --openmp a.f90 -o x.omp --openmp-lib-dir=$CONDA_PREFIX/lib
```
But the following runs almost instantly (0.4s), so this is almost for sure a bug with OpenMP source code:
```
lfortran --cpp  --openmp a.f90 -o x.omp --openmp-lib-dir=$CONDA_PREFIX/lib
```

Bugs to isolate and fix:
* [ ] Segfault in Release mode
* [ ] Better error message in Debug mode
* [ ] User (pure) function inside `do concurrent` body
* [ ] Fix OpenMP input to run like `do concurrent`, something is wrong there
* [ ] Also fix https://github.com/lfortran/lfortran/issues/5870 and https://github.com/lfortran/lfortran/issues/4248 so that no workarounds are MRE 

```fortran

program openmp_43
USE iso_fortran_env, ONLY: real64
integer :: i, j, res=0
do concurrent ( i =1:4)
    call SUB(i)
    print *, FUN(i)
end do 

CONTAINS

subroutine SUB(x)
    integer, intent(in) :: x
    print *,x*x
end subroutine SUB

REAL(real64) FUNCTION FUN(x)
    integer, intent(in) :: x
    integer, intent(out) :: y
    y=x*x
end function FUN
end program

```

For Function

Now the Error in CI test
```console
[100%] Building Fortran object CMakeFiles/fortran_01.dir/fortran_01.f90.o
CMakeFiles/fortran_01.dir/fortran_01.f90.o.tmp.f90:57:6:

   57 | ptr = c_loc(x)
      |      1
Error: Cannot convert INTEGER(4) to TYPE(c_ptr) at (1)
```

As per this [comment](https://github.com/lfortran/lfortran/pull/5340#pullrequestreview-2427272474) 
The problem here is that 
We are assigning `INTEGER(4)`  (which is address ) to the `TYPE(c_ptr)` which is like user defined struct which have member named `ptr`

It can be seen here 
```fortran
module lfortran_intrinsic_iso_c_binding
implicit none
type :: c_funptr
    integer(4) :: ptr
end type c_funptr
type :: c_ptr
    integer(4) :: ptr        ! This is the main Line
end type c_ptr
integer(4), parameter :: c_bool = 4
integer(4), parameter :: c_char = 1
integer(4), parameter :: c_double = 8
integer(4), parameter :: c_double_complex = 8
integer(4), parameter :: c_float = 4
integer(4), parameter :: c_float_complex = 4
integer(4), parameter :: c_int = 4
integer(4), parameter :: c_int16_t = 2
integer(4), parameter :: c_int32_t = 4
integer(4), parameter :: c_int64_t = 8
integer(4), parameter :: c_int8_t = 1
integer(4), parameter :: c_long = 4
integer(4), parameter :: c_long_double = -1
integer(4), parameter :: c_long_double_complex = -1
integer(4), parameter :: c_long_long = 8
character(len=1, kind=1), parameter :: c_null_char = ""
type(c_ptr), parameter :: c_null_ptr = c_ptr(0)
integer(4), parameter :: c_short = 2
integer(4), parameter :: c_size_t = 8
interface
    logical(4) function c_associated(c_ptr_1)
        import c_ptr
        type(c_ptr), intent(in) :: c_ptr_1
    end function c_associated

    subroutine c_f_pointer(cptr, fptr, shape)
        import c_ptr
        type(c_ptr), intent(in) :: cptr
        integer(4), pointer, intent(out) :: fptr
        integer(4), dimension(:), intent(in), optional :: shape
    end subroutine c_f_pointer

    integer(4) function c_funloc(x)
        integer(4), intent(in) :: x
    end function c_funloc

    integer(4) function c_loc(x)
        integer(4), intent(in) :: x
    end function c_loc
end interface
end module lfortran_intrinsic_iso_c_binding

program fortran_01
use lfortran_intrinsic_iso_c_binding, only: c_loc
use lfortran_intrinsic_iso_c_binding, only: c_ptr
implicit none
type(c_ptr) :: ptr
integer(4), pointer :: x
ptr = c_loc(x)
end program fortran_01
```

1. There should not be any intermediate statements between inner nested loops.
2. Therefore, we should only be concerned about the body of the last nested loop.

For more information, refer to the article on [Coding Habits](https://www.admin-magazine.com/HPC/Articles/OpenMP-Coding-Habits-and-GPUs).

Additionally, I checked various other sources, and developers typically use the `collapse` clause with the following assumptions:
- The iterations will be independent of each other.
- There won't be any intermediate code between loops.","reactions":{"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/lfortran/lfortran/issues/comments/2415801274/reactions","total_count":0,"+1":0,"-1":0,"laugh":0,"hooray":0,"confused":0,"heart":0,"rocket":0,"eyes":0},"performed_via_github_app":null}}

Let's say for example
we have code
```fortran
!$omp parallel do collapse(3) private(iy, ix, iz)
do ix = 1, nx
    do iy = 1, ny
        do iz = 1, nz
            print *, "iy->", iy, "ix->", ix, "iz->", iz
        end do
    end do
end do
!$omp end parallel do
```

Can be easily converted to the 
do concurrent ( ix =1:nx, iy = 1:ny, iz=1:nz )
print *, "iy->", iy, "ix->", ix, "iz->", iz
end do","reactions":{"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/lfortran/lfortran/issues/comments/2415808378/reactions","total_count":0,"+1":0,"-1":0,"laugh":0,"hooray":0,"confused":0,"heart":0,"rocket":0,"eyes":0},"performed_via_github_app":null}}

```fortran
!$omp parallel do collapse(2) private(iy, ix, iz)
do ix = 1, nx
    do iy = 1, ny
        do iz = 1, nz
            print *, "iy->", iy, "ix->", ix, "iz->", iz
        end do
    end do
end do
!$omp end parallel do
```

can be interppreted as
```fortran
do concurrent ( ix =1:nx, iy = 1:ny )
do iz=1,nz
print *, "iy->", iy, "ix->", ix, "iz->", iz
end do
end do
~``","reactions":{"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/lfortran/lfortran/issues/comments/2415811234/reactions","total_count":0,"+1":0,"-1":0,"laugh":0,"hooray":0,"confused":0,"heart":0,"rocket":0,"eyes":0},"performed_via_github_app":null}}

Compilation of [Prima](https://github.com/libprima/prima)
gfortran is able to handle this section of file `prima/fortran/common/consts.F90`

```fortran
integer, parameter :: IK_DFT = kind(0)  ! Default integer kind
integer, parameter :: RP_DFT = kind(0.0)  ! Default real kind

! Define the integer kind to be used in the Fortran code.
#if PRIMA_INTEGER_KIND  == 0
integer, parameter :: IK = IK_DFT
#elif PRIMA_INTEGER_KIND == 16
integer, parameter :: IK = INT16
#elif PRIMA_INTEGER_KIND == 32
integer, parameter :: IK = INT32
#elif PRIMA_INTEGER_KIND == 64
integer, parameter :: IK = INT64
#else
integer, parameter :: IK = IK_DFT
#endif
```
But both GFortran and LFortran Fails to run this MRE 
```fortran

#elif a == 2
    print *, 'msg2'
#else
    print *, 'Unknown number'
#endif

end program example
```

```fortran
program main
   use omp_lib
! declare variables as required
   integer :: ny, nx, nz, nk
   integer :: iy, ix, iz, ik
   ny=3
   nx=4
   nz=5
   nk=6

!$omp parallel do private(iy, ix, iz)
   do iy = 1, ny

    !$omp parallel do collapse(2)
      do ix = 1, nx
         do ik = 1, nk
            print *,"iy->", iy,"ix->", ix
         end do
      end do
    !$omp end parallel do

    !$omp parallel do
      do ix = 1, nx
         do ik = 1, nk
            print *,"iy->", iy,"ix->", ix
         end do
      end do
    !$omp end parallel do
      
   end do
!$omp end parallel do

end program

> 
> ```fortran

> integer :: ix, iy, iz
> integer, parameter :: nx = 10, ny = 10, nz = 10
> integer :: r1, r2
> 
> !$omp parallel do collapse(3) shared(r1,r2) private(iy, ix, iz)
> do ix = 1, nx
>     do iy = 1, ny
>         r2 = ix * iy
>         do iz = 1, nz
>                 r1 = ix + iy + iz
>         end do
>     end do
> end do
> !$omp end parallel do
> 
> end program main
> ```

o a

{"action":"created","comment":{"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/lfortran/lfortran/pulls/comments/1759968035","pull_request_review_id":2305287135,"id":1759968035,"node_id":"PRRC_kwDOCpMuHc5o5vsj","diff_hunk":"@@ -0,0 +1,25 @@
+subroutine omp_func(n)
+    use omp_lib
+implicit none
+integer, intent(in) :: n
+integer :: i
+call omp_set_num_threads(5)
+!$omp parallel  private(i) shared(n)
+!$omp do
+do i = 1, n
+    !omp critical
+    print *,omp_get_thread_num()
+    print *, "xyz"
+end do
+!$omp end do
+!$omp end parallel
+
+print *, "n = ", n
+if (n /= 10) error stop
+end subroutine
+

[Issue](https://github.com/lfortran/lfortran/issues/4900)
To implement multiple control statements in the DoConcurrent loop, we need to first modify the grammar to:  
`| DoConcurrentLoop(do_loop_head* head, expr* shared, expr* local, reduction_expr* reduction, stmt* body)`  
Next, update all references to `make_DoConcurrentLoop_t` as suggested by @Pranavchiku. We will retain `do_loop_head_t* m_head` in the `DoConcurrentLoop_t` struct and the `make_DoConcurrentLoop_t` definition. However, updating the references in `asr.h` causes the type to revert, leading to build errors in the following function:  
- `ASR::asr_t* duplicate_DoConcurrentLoop(DoConcurrentLoop_t* x)`

### Current Changes Made:
- Updated **ASR.asdl** to:  
  `| DoConcurrentLoop(do_loop_head* head, expr* shared, expr* local, reduction_expr* reduction, stmt* body)`

### Primary Steps to Consider:
- Modify `asdl_cpp.py` to correctly generate `asr.h`
- Update `asr.h` and `ast_body_visitor` wherever `make_DoConcurrentLoop_t` is referenced

Before proceeding with the first step, we should consider two options:

1. **Changes in `asdl_cpp.py`**: 
   - Modifications were made in the respective visitor classes:
     - `ExprStmtDuplicatorVisitor`
     - `DeserializationVisitorVisitor`
     
2. **Functions Updated**: 
   - Two functions were using `make_DoConcurrentLoop_t`: 
     - `ASR::asr_t* duplicate_DoConcurrentLoop`
     - `asr_t* deserialize_DoLoop()`



3. **Struct Update**: 
   - Changed `ASNodeVisitor::visitConstructor` in `asdl_cpp.py` to include:
     - `Vec<do_loop_head_t> m_head;`
